dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won on breadth and tone: Claude Code had a full front-page discussion about how to use it safely with scoped credentials, and other coding threads repeatedly treated Anthropic models as the reference point for strong coding assistance [E1][E2][E3].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the strongest day because its mentions were both more numerous and more favorable than any other tracked provider. One story was directly about operating Claude Code safely in real workflows, while separate threads praised Claude’s usefulness for testing and treated Anthropic models as the baseline that competing coding tools were measured against [E1][E2][E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: GPT-5.2 drew wary attention for clever exploit-generation behavior in a security thread, while Codex also got a clear quality compliment that was tempered by complaints about long wait times [E4][E5].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code stayed prominent as developers discussed safer ways to give it access, and separate coding threads treated Anthropic models as a strong default for code assistance and testing work [E1][E2][E3].

evidence.summary: The front-page story 'Using proxies to hide secrets from Claude Code' prompted practical discussion about scoped credentials, proxies, and Anthropic API keys, showing active Claude Code use but centering on access control rather than clear praise or backlash.
In the GLM-4.7-Flash thread, commenters said claude-code is designed to work much better with Anthropic models and used Haiku/Sonnet as the reference quality bar.
A top comment in 'Provide agents with automated feedback' said Claude is pretty good at suggesting interesting properties to test and writing property tests.

The exploit-generation thread highlighted GPT-5.2 finding a clever seven-function-call path to write a file under heavy protections, but the immediate reaction was 'Yikes' and broader concern about scary offensive capability.
In the GLM-4.7-Flash discussion, a commenter said Codex is notably higher quality, though they also complained that it makes them wait too long.
